segments.speaker: EMPLOYEE
segments.en: Hello, Picturerep.
segments.zh: 您好，这里是Picturerep。有什么可以帮您的吗？

segments.speaker: EMPLOYEE
segments.en: Can I help you?

segments.speaker: WOMAN
segments.en: Oh, hi.
segments.zh: 哦，你好。我看到你们关于把照片复制到光盘上的广告，我想多了解一些你们的具体服务。

segments.speaker: WOMAN
segments.en: I saw your advertisement about copying pictures to disk and I'd like a bit more information about what you do.

segments.speaker: EMPLOYEE
segments.en: Sure.
segments.zh: 当然。您想知道什么？

segments.speaker: EMPLOYEE
segments.en: What would you like to know?

segments.speaker: WOMAN
segments.en: Well, I've got a box full of old family photos that's been up in the attic for years, some of them must be 50 or 60 years old, and I'd like to get them converted to digital format.
segments.zh: 嗯，我有一盒子老家庭照片，在阁楼上放了好多年了，有些可能都有五六十年了，我想把它们转换成数字格式。

segments.speaker: EMPLOYEE
segments.en: Sure, we can do that for you.
segments.zh: 没问题，我们可以帮您处理。

segments.speaker: WOMAN
segments.en: Right.
segments.zh: 好的。那尺寸呢？照片各种尺寸都有——有什么限制吗？

segments.speaker: WOMAN
segments.en: And what about size?

segments.speaker: WOMAN
segments.en: The photos are all sorts of sizes – are there any restrictions?

segments.speaker: EMPLOYEE
segments.en: Well the maximum size of photo we can do with our normal service is 30 centimetres.
segments.zh: 嗯，我们常规服务能处理的最大照片尺寸是30厘米。每张照片至少要有4厘米，这是我们能处理的最小尺寸。

segments.speaker: EMPLOYEE
segments.en: And each picture must be at least 4 centimetres, that's the minimum we can cope with.

segments.speaker: WOMAN
segments.en: That should be fine.
segments.zh: 那应该没问题。有些照片是带相框的——我寄过去之前需要把它们取出来吗？

segments.speaker: WOMAN
segments.en: And some of them are in a frame – should I take them out before I send them?

segments.speaker: EMPLOYEE
segments.en: Yes please, we can't copy them otherwise.
segments.zh: 是的，请把照片分开，否则我们没法复印。而且所有照片必须单独一张张的，不能贴在相册里。

segments.speaker: EMPLOYEE
segments.en: And also the photos must all be separate, they mustn't be stuck into an album.

segments.speaker: WOMAN
segments.en: OK, that's not a problem.
segments.zh: 好的，没问题。那你能告诉我大概要多少钱吗？我大概有360张照片。

segments.speaker: WOMAN
segments.en: So can you give me an idea of how much this will cost?

segments.speaker: WOMAN
segments.en: I've got about 360 photos I think.

segments.speaker: EMPLOYEE
segments.en: We charge £195 for 300 to 400 photos for the basic service.
segments.zh: 基本服务的话，300到400张照片我们收费195英镑。

segments.speaker: WOMAN
segments.en: OK.
segments.zh: 好的。那这个费用包括光盘吗？

segments.speaker: WOMAN
segments.en: And does that include the disk?

segments.speaker: EMPLOYEE
segments.en: Yes, one disk – but you can get extra ones for £5 each.
segments.zh: 是的，包含一张光盘——但你可以额外购买，每张5英镑。

segments.speaker: WOMAN
segments.en: That's good.
segments.zh: 那太好了。我寄照片的时候就需要付款吗？

segments.speaker: WOMAN
segments.en: So do I need to pay when I send you the photos?

segments.speaker: EMPLOYEE
segments.en: No, we won't need anything until we've actually copied the pictures.
segments.zh: 不用，我们直到复印完照片才会收费。到时候我们会通知您费用，收到付款后，我们就会把包裹寄给您。

segments.speaker: EMPLOYEE
segments.en: Then we'll let you know how much it is, and once we've received the payment, we'll send the parcel off to you.

segments.speaker: WOMAN
segments.en: Right.
segments.zh: 好的。

segments.speaker: EMPLOYEE
segments.en: Is there anything else you'd like to ask about our services?
segments.zh: 您还有什么想了解我们的服务吗？

segments.speaker: WOMAN
segments.en: Yes.
segments.zh: 有的。我大致按内容把照片分成了几组——你们复制的时候能保持这些分组吗？

segments.speaker: WOMAN
segments.en: I've roughly sorted out the photos into groups, according to what they're about – so can you keep them in those groups when you copy them?

segments.speaker: EMPLOYEE
segments.en: Sure.
segments.zh: 当然。我们会把每组照片分别存在光盘的不同文件夹里，如果您愿意，还可以给每个文件夹起个名字。

segments.speaker: EMPLOYEE
segments.en: We'll save each group in a different folder on the disk and if you like, you can suggest a name for each folder.

segments.speaker: WOMAN
segments.en: So I could have one called 'Grandparents' for instance?
segments.zh: 比如我可以有一个叫“祖父母”的文件夹？

segments.speaker: EMPLOYEE
segments.en: Exactly.
segments.zh: 没错。

segments.speaker: WOMAN
segments.en: And do you do anything besides scan the photos?
segments.zh: 除了扫描照片，你们还做别的吗？比如，能做些优化吗？

segments.speaker: WOMAN
segments.en: Like, can you make any improvements?

segments.speaker: EMPLOYEE
segments.en: Yes, in the standard service each photo is checked, and we can sometimes touch up the colour a bit, or improve the contrast – that can make a big difference.
segments.zh: 有的，在标准服务中，每张照片都会经过检查，我们有时会稍微调整颜色或提高对比度——效果会很不一样。

segments.speaker: WOMAN
segments.en: OK.
segments.zh: 好的。有些照片其实很脆弱——处理过程中不会损坏吧？

segments.speaker: WOMAN
segments.en: And some of the photos are actually quite fragile – they won't get damaged in the process, will they?

segments.speaker: EMPLOYEE
segments.en: No, if any look particularly fragile, we'd do them by hand.
segments.zh: 不，如果有些看起来特别易碎，我们会手工处理。我们很清楚这些老照片有多珍贵。

segments.speaker: EMPLOYEE
segments.en: We do realise how precious these old photos can be.

segments.speaker: WOMAN
segments.en: Sure.
segments.zh: 当然。

segments.speaker: EMPLOYEE
segments.en: And another thing is we can make changes to a photo if you want – so if you want to remove an object from a photo, or maybe alter the background, we can do that.
segments.zh: 还有一件事，如果您愿意，我们可以对照片进行修改——比如移除照片中的某个物体，或者改变背景，我们都能做。

segments.speaker: WOMAN
segments.en: Really?
segments.zh: 真的吗？我可能会感兴趣。我会翻翻照片看看。哦，说到修复照片——我有几张对焦不准的。你们能处理一下吗？

segments.speaker: WOMAN
segments.en: I might be interested in that.

segments.speaker: WOMAN
segments.en: I'll have a look through the photos and see.

segments.speaker: WOMAN
segments.en: Oh, and talking of fixing photos – I've got a few that aren't properly in focus.

segments.speaker: WOMAN
segments.en: Can you do anything to make that better?

segments.speaker: EMPLOYEE
segments.en: No, I'm afraid that's one thing we can't do.
segments.zh: 不，恐怕这是我们做不到的。

segments.speaker: WOMAN
segments.en: OK.
segments.zh: 好的。

segments.speaker: EMPLOYEE
segments.en: Any other information I can give you?
segments.zh: 还有什么其他信息需要我提供吗？

segments.speaker: WOMAN
segments.en: Er ... oh, how long will it all take?
segments.zh: 嗯……哦，整个过程需要多长时间？

segments.speaker: EMPLOYEE
segments.en: We aim to get the copying done in ten days.
segments.zh: 我们争取在十天内完成复印。

segments.speaker: WOMAN
segments.en: Fine.
segments.zh: 好的。那我们把照片装进盒子里寄给你。

segments.speaker: WOMAN
segments.en: Right, we'll get the photos packed up in a box and post them off to you.

segments.speaker: EMPLOYEE
segments.en: Right.
segments.zh: 好的。如果你们有结实的纸板箱，那是最好的。我们发现塑料箱有时会在邮寄过程中破损。

segments.speaker: EMPLOYEE
segments.en: If you've got a strong cardboard box, that's best.

segments.speaker: EMPLOYEE
segments.en: We've found that plastic ones sometimes break in the post.

segments.speaker: WOMAN
segments.en: OK.
segments.zh: 好的。谢谢你的帮助。再见。

segments.speaker: WOMAN
segments.en: Right, thanks for your help.

segments.speaker: WOMAN
segments.en: Bye.

segments.speaker: EMPLOYEE
segments.en: Bye.
segments.zh: 再见。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Good morning and thank you for coming here today.
segments.zh:
早上好，感谢大家今天来到这里。我想向大家介绍一下学校的最新变化，这些变化将影响到您的孩子。
如您所知，学校的建筑建于不同时期：有些是20世纪70年代的，有些是近五年的，当然达特菲尔德庄园已有一个多世纪的历史了。它是由商人内维尔·理查兹委托建造的，原计划作为他的家族住宅，但他去世时尚未完工。他的继承人选择将其出售给当地议会，议会将其改造成了办公室。后来计划将其改造成旅游信息中心，但因资金不足未能实现，反而在40年前学校开办时成为了学校的核心建筑。
随着当地人口的增长，学校也在扩大。现在我可以向大家介绍一些关于低年级校区的情况，该校区与主校区隔着一条马路。两个校区的开发规划已获批准。低年级将搬迁到主校区新建的校舍中。开发商将在现有的低年级校区建造房屋。新校舍的建设工作应在未来几个月内开始。
一个更近期的变化涉及餐饮设施和食堂。食堂在午餐时段总是非常繁忙——实际上常常满员，因为我们很多学生喜欢那里提供的食物。但只有一个服务点，所以大多数学生需要等待相当长的时间才能取餐。这显然不尽如人意，因为他们可能刚吃完午饭，下午的课就开始了。
因此，我们新建了一个美食厅，将于下周投入使用。它将设有多个服务区，稍后我会详细介绍。但为了美食厅的顺利运行，我们请求您每天早上与孩子讨论他们当天想吃什么类型的食物，这样他们可以直接前往相应的服务点。那里不会有垃圾食品——所有提供的食物都是健康的——而且午餐付费方式不变，仍是通过在线充值孩子的电子支付卡。
您可能想知道旧食堂会怎么处理。那里仍然会摆放桌椅，学生可以吃从美食厅购买的食物或从家里带来的午餐。最终我们可能会将部分食堂用作储藏室，但首先我们会看看午餐时间有多少学生去那里。

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: I'd like to bring you up to date with changes in the school that will affect your children.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: As you know, the school buildings date from various times: some from the 1970s, some from the last five years, and of course Dartfield House is over a century old.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: It was commissioned by a businessman, Neville Richards, and intended as his family home, but he died before it was completed.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: His heir chose to sell it to the local council, who turned it into offices.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: A later plan to convert it into a tourist information centre didn't come about, through lack of money, and instead it formed the nucleus of this school when it opened 40 years ago.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: The school has grown as the local population has increased, and I can now give you some news about the lower school site, which is separated from the main site by a road.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Planning permission has been granted for development of both sites.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: The lower school will move to new buildings that will be constructed on the main site.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Developers will construct houses on the existing lower school site.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Work on the new school buildings should start within the next few months.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: A more imminent change concerns the catering facilities and the canteen.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: The canteen is always very busy throughout the lunch period – in fact it's often full to capacity, because a lot of our pupils like the food that's on offer there.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: But there's only one serving point, so most pupils have to wait a considerable time to be served.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: This is obviously unsatisfactory, as they may have hardly finished their lunch before afternoon lessons start.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: So we've had a new Food Hall built, and this will come into use next week.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: It'll have several serving areas, and I'll give you more details about those in a minute, but one thing we ask you to do, to help in the smooth running of the Food Hall, is to discuss with your children each morning which type of food they want to eat that day, so they can go straight to the relevant serving point.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: There won't be any junk food – everything on offer will be healthy – and there's no change to the current system of paying for lunches by topping up your child's electronic payment card online.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: You may be wondering what will happen to the old canteen.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: We'll still have tables and chairs in there, and pupils can eat food from the Food Hall or lunch they've brought from home.

segments.speaker: LECTURER
segments.en: Eventually we may use part of the canteen for storage, but first we'll see how many pupils go in there at lunchtime.

items.prompt: Complete the notes below. Write ONE WORD AND/OR A NUMBER for each answer.

pairs.note: 题干中的frame与原文中的frame完全一致，直接定位。

paraphrase.explain: 题干要求填写一个单词或数字，原文中出现了frame，且与答案一致，直接定位到该词。
